concerns.text: SC-003 specifies Monte Carlo validation threshold ≤ 0.01, while FR-026 and SC-026 specify ≤ 0.005. This creates a conflicting requirement and makes SC-003 untestable.

FR-002 references 'data-model.md' and 'extracted_summary.schema.yaml' which are external files not included in the spec. These references should either be self-contained or clearly indicate how to access these external dependencies.
FR-030 cites 'Kohavi et al. (2020)' but the reference lacks full bibliographic details. Constitution Principle II requires verified citations with title-token-overlap ≥ 0.7.
SC-003 specifies Monte Carlo validation threshold ≤ 0.01, but FR-026 (which SC-026 anchors to) specifies ≤ 0.005. This creates an untestable contradiction – which threshold governs verification?
SC-026 references FR-026, but does not resolve the threshold conflict between SC-003 and FR-026. The success criteria should align with the functional requirements.
